candidates.why: Primary operating revenue line; matches top-line sales and equals Total Revenue figures.
Explicit total revenue line with same figures as Operating revenue; included as fallback if label changes over time.
Direct cost of materials clearly represents cost of goods sold / cost of sales.
Includes multiple operating cost components, but is an aggregate that also bundles SG&A and other costs; not ideal for pure COGS.
Typical SG&A-type overhead (selling, admin, other operating costs) excluding COGS, D&A, and interest.
Aggregate of multiple operating costs including COGS; excluded to avoid mixing COGS with SG&A per instructions.
Explicit R&D expense line; best match for research and development expense.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: Related to R&D but it is amortization of R&D intangibles, not current-period R&D expense; secondary at best.
Primary interest expense line; best representation of financing cost.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Component of interest related to lease liabilities; useful if main Interest Expense excludes it.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; included as an additional fallback label.
Aggregate net interest line; kept as lower-priority fallback if granular interest expense lines are missing in some years.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

candidates.why: Represents equity attributable to shareholders (parent), separate from non-controlling interest which is listed below; ideal for book equity excluding NCI.
Represents total equity including non-controlling interests (matches Total equity + NCI); not used for BE to avoid double counting with MIB.
Balance sheet line for non-controlling interests; primary MIB component to pair with parent equity.
Alternative label for the same concept; included as fallback if naming changes over time.
